meaning: adj.不熟练的,拙劣的,无需技能的
examples: Most car thieves are opportunist unskilled petty criminals; many are under 20.
At first, thousands of unskilled laborers were recruited; by the 1730's, however, American employers demanded skilled artisans.
He worked as an unskilled labourer.
With so many young, unskilled workers looking for work, employers can pick and choose.
Permit freer trade in skilled labor (and pathways for unskilled for that matter): Bright foreign Ph.
